pages.text: One sunny morning, <name> went to the village fair with Amma and Papa. The air was full of laughter, music, and the smell of sweet jalebis.
“Look, Papa!” <name> pointed. “The ferris wheel is a big round circle!” Papa smiled. “Yes, <name>! And it goes round and round!”
At the balloon stall, a kind balloon seller waved. “Would you like a triangle kite balloon or a heart one?” he asked. <name> giggled. “One of each, please!”

Next, they stopped at the sweet shop. Shiny square boxes of ladoos and star-shaped barfis glittered on the counter. “Mmm, sweet stars!” said <name>. The shopkeeper chuckled. “Stars taste best when you share them!”
Nearby, a juggler tossed oval lemons in the air — flip, flop, fly! — while colorful rectangular carpets lay on the ground. “So many shapes, Amma!” said <name>.
Amma nodded. “Shapes make the world beautiful, don’t they?” As the sun set, lanterns shaped like diamonds floated above the mela. <name> clapped her hands. “Everywhere I look, I see a shape!”

Papa lifted her up. “That’s right, my little explorer. Learning shapes makes every day an adventure!” <name> smiled, hugging her balloon. The fair sparkled with colors and shapes — round, square, triangle, and star — shining together like magic.
